channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Voters to receive postcards for District 1 special primary election
item.description: Registered voters in San Diego County's First Supervisorial District will soon receive postcards for the April 8 special primary election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Florida House and Senate leaders take charge in special session showdown
item.description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis pushed for a special session to help boost President Donald Trump's immigration efforts. But that session was blocked by Senate and House leadership Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu hopes to meet Trump in Washington as soon as next week, US officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is hoping to meet with President Donald Trump in Washington as early as next week, according to two U.S. officials familiar with preliminary planning for the trip.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US places dozens of senior aid officials on leave, citing possible resistance to Trump orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At least 56 senior officials in the top U.S. aid and development agency were placed on leave Monday amid an investigation into an alleged effort to thwart President Donald Trump’s orders. A current official and a former official at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department said Monday that it had fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal prosecutions of President Donald Trump, moving rapidly to pursue retribution against lawyers involved in the investigations and signaling an early willingness to take action favo
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court leaves in place Mississippi’s voting ban for people convicted of some crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday left in place Mississippi’s Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First lady Melania Trump’s new official portrait revealed
item.description: Melania Trump's new official White House portrait was released on Monday. The first lady is all business in the black and white picture, wearing a suit while striking a power pose.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department fires more than a dozen key officials on former Special Counsel Jack Smith's team
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: The Justice Department is firing more than a dozen key officials who worked on Special Counsel Jack Smith’s team to prosecute President Trump, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth, Britt accuse Air Force of 'malicious' pause as it reinstates training on Tuskegee Airmen
item.description: The Air Force has reinstated a course on the first Black pilots unit after it was yanked to comply with President Donald Trump’s executive order banning DEI in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DAs may try to charge Jan. 6 participants granted clemency by Trump with new crimes on state, local levels
item.description: The ordeal may not be over for some of the more than 1,500 Jan. 6 criminal defendants granted clemency by President Donald Trump — as certain prosecutors are investigating charges at the state level.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's celebration of American greatness puts a spotlight on a little-known panel of experts
item.description: Within hours of taking office, President Donald Trump outlined in one of his many executive orders a mission to celebrate American greatness and to recognize those who have made contributions throughout history.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems take ‘government for granted’ on immigration: Chicago alderman
item.description: "Many Democratic leaders refuse to show any kind of working relationship with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Ramaswamy's expected run for Ohio governor would test experienced Republicans and tradition
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Fresh off of his unexpected departure from President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, Vivek Ramaswamy has set his sights on becoming governor of his home state.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump reverses Biden's ban on private prison contracts
item.description: Trump's action comes after signals from private prison corporations like GEO Group and CoreCivic that they expected such a reversal.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Bessent nomination to lead Treasury
item.description: The Senate on Saturday voted to advance Scott Bessent’s nomination to become the next secretary of the Treasury, putting him in line to be confirmed on Monday. Senators voted 67-23 to tee up final passage on Bessent’s nomination. The final vote will take place Monday evening at 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump has tossed a lot of Biden policies. But there’s one AI measure he wants to keep
item.description: President Trump says he likes Biden’s idea to open up federal lands for AI data centers. His White House is looking for ways to ensure U.S. dominance in the sector.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Top Minnesota attorney: Birthright citizenship suit against Trump just the beginning
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship two days after Minnesota joined 21 other states in a lawsuit to challenge it. Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison discussed his strategy.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: New York plan would give money to new moms: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York would give hundreds of dollars in financial support to low-income parents under a new proposal. Last week, Gov. Kathy Hochul announced a proposal to give low-income parents a “birth allowance” during pregnancy and at the birth of their child.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada, 17 other states sue Trump administration over birthright citizenship order
item.description: Attorneys general from 18 states, including Nevada, sued the Trump administration Tuesday to stop it from seeking to eliminate birthright citizenship, a protection guaranteed in the U.S. Constitution.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump wants to pull US out of World Health Organization again; here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11
